schools.summary: Gill: Timnath fell by lot to Judah, then to Dan, and was now in the hands of the Philistines; he saw a woman of the Philistines.

themes: And Samson went down to Timnath, and saw a woman in Timnath of the daughters of the Philistines
the peril of the unguarded sight leading the heart astray
I made a covenant with mine eyes; why then should I think upon a maid? (Job 31:1)

schools.summary: Gill: he told them, desiring their approbation and consent, acting a dutiful part, an example to children to advise with parents.

themes: he... told his father and his mother... now therefore get her for me to wife
the duty to seek parents' counsel, yet a will fixed on the unlawful
children, obey your parents in the Lord: for this is right (Eph. 6:1)

schools.summary: Gill: their expected objection.
Clarke: to marry any not of the Israelitish stock was contrary to the law.

themes: Is there never a woman among the daughters of thy brethren... that thou goest to take a wife of the uncircumcised Philistines?
parents warning against the unequal yoke forbidden by God's law
be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers (2 Cor. 6:14)

schools.summary: Gill: Samson knew it was of the Lord, his desire not arising from carnal affection merely; the Philistines had dominion over Israel.

debates: Was Samson's choice his fault or God's leading?

The witnesses note the tension: the marriage was contrary to the law and sprang from Samson's own desire, yet 'it was of the LORD' — God overruling his inclination for a deliverance purpose. Gill holds Samson acted under a divine impulse; others stress that God overruled a real fault. Either way the providence does not excuse the unequal yoke.
themes: his father and his mother knew not that it was of the LORD, that he sought an occasion against the Philistines
the mystery of providence overruling men's course to God's purpose
surely the wrath of man shall praise thee (Ps. 76:10)

schools.summary: Gill: the parents were prevailed upon to go, his affections being so set on the marriage; a young lion roared against him.

themes: a young lion roared against him
the people of God meeting the roaring foe in unexpected places
your adversary the devil, as a roaring lion, walketh about, seeking whom he may devour (1 Pet. 5:8)

schools.summary: Gill: the Spirit of might from the Lord, inspiring courage and increasing his bodily strength.
Keil & Delitzsch: he rent the lion as a kid.

themes: the Spirit of the LORD came mightily upon him, and he rent him as he would have rent a kid
Samson's strength a gift of the Spirit, the weak made mighty
be strong in the Lord, and in the power of his might (Eph. 6:10)

schools.summary: Gill: he entered conversation to judge her other qualifications besides outward form; she pleased Samson well.

themes: he went down, and talked with the woman; and she pleased Samson well
the desire of the flesh and eye governing the weightiest choices
the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes... is not of the Father (1 John 2:16)

schools.summary: Gill: matters being settled and the espousals made, he returned at the time for the marriage; there was a swarm of bees and honey in the carcase.

themes: he turned aside to see the carcase of the lion: and, behold, there was a swarm of bees and honey in the carcase
God bringing sweetness out of the bitter, life out of death
O death, where is thy sting?... thanks be to God, which giveth us the victory (1 Cor. 15:55-57)

schools.summary: Gill: Josephus says he took pieces of the honeycomb and ate as he went; he told them not he had taken it from the lion's carcase.

themes: he gave them, and they did eat: but he told not them that he had taken the honey out of the carcase of the lion
things wisely kept for a season, yet discretion in walking with God
a prudent man concealeth knowledge (Prov. 12:23)

schools.summary: Gill: the father went down about the consummation of the marriage; Samson made a feast, as the young men used to do.

themes: Samson made there a feast; for so used the young men to do
lawful customs kept, yet beware the company among the ungodly
evil communications corrupt good manners (1 Cor. 15:33)

schools.summary: Gill: the Philistines of Timnath brought them when they saw he was come to consummate his marriage.
Keil & Delitzsch: the thirty companions.

themes: when they saw him, that they brought thirty companions to be with him
the unequal yoke compassing the believer with the world's company
I have hated the congregation of evil doers (Ps. 26:4-5)

schools.summary: Gill: he said it to his thirty companions, very likely on the first day of the feast.
Keil & Delitzsch: the riddle and the wager.

themes: I will now put forth a riddle unto you... then I will give you thirty sheets and thirty change of garments
God turning even a feast's diversion to his purpose
to understand a proverb... the words of the wise, and their dark sayings (Prov. 1:6)

schools.summary: Gill: if they could not explain it in the time allowed.
Keil & Delitzsch: their reply, Put forth thy riddle.

themes: if ye cannot declare it me, then shall ye give me thirty sheets... Put forth thy riddle, that we may hear it
the cleverness of the world trusting in itself
the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God (1 Cor. 3:19)

schools.summary: Gill: out of a devouring eater, such as the lion, came forth honey, which Samson and his parents ate.

themes: Out of the eater came forth meat, and out of the strong came forth sweetness
a shadow of the gospel: sweetness and life out of the Strong One's conquest of death
through death he might destroy him that had the power of death (Heb. 2:14)

schools.summary: Gill: they applied to his wife some time before the seventh day, pressing her hard, threatening to burn her and her father's house.

themes: Entice thy husband, that he may declare unto us the riddle, lest we burn thee and thy father's house with fire
the world resorting to threats and treachery when fair means fail
they bend their tongues like their bow for lies (Jer. 9:3)

schools.summary: Gill: she came to get the explanation, thinking her tears would move him.
Keil & Delitzsch: Samson's wife wept before him.

themes: Samson's wife wept before him, and said, Thou dost but hate me, and lovest me not
the importunity and tears of an unbelieving partner a snare
the lips of a strange woman drop as an honeycomb... but her end is bitter (Prov. 5:3-4)

schools.summary: Gill: she wept the remaining days, lying sore upon him; he told her, and she told the riddle to her people.

themes: she wept before him the seven days... he told her, because she lay sore upon him: and she told the riddle to the children of her people
the peril of yielding to the wearing importunity of the ungodly
give not that which is holy unto the dogs (Matt. 7:6)

schools.summary: Gill: they answered before sundown, soon enough to free themselves from the obligation; Samson said, if ye had not plowed with my heifer.

themes: If ye had not plowed with my heifer, ye had not found out my riddle
ill-gotten gains no true victory; the world's treachery exposed
bread of deceit is sweet... but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel (Prov. 20:17)

schools.summary: Gill: the Spirit of might from the Lord filled him with zeal and courage and increased his strength.
Keil & Delitzsch: he gave the changes to them that expounded the riddle.

themes: the Spirit of the LORD came upon him, and he went down to Ashkelon, and slew thirty men of them, and took their spoil
God turning the affront into a stroke against the Philistines
he taketh the wise in their own craftiness (Job 5:13)

schools.summary: Gill: she was given by her father, with her consent, both being provoked by Samson leaving her, to his companion.

themes: But Samson's wife was given to his companion, whom he had used as his friend
the unequal yoke ending in grief and betrayal
mine own familiar friend... hath lifted up his heel against me (Ps. 41:9)
